tickets.title: S5 Audit: Credentials in auth-profiles.json + Ollama Cloud provider config
tickets.description: S5 control (no hardcoded creds). Findings: (1) RESOLVED: Old rotated Anthropic key in backups/gateway-config/2026-04-27/ sanitised (CHG pending). (2) OPEN: All agent auth-profiles.json store API keys as plaintext JSON. Need: chmod 600 on all agentDir/, verify OpenClaw SecretRef support, update backup-exclusion policy. (3) OPEN: Ollama Cloud models not configured as OpenClaw provider. kimi/deepseek only work via ollama CLI, not OpenClaw model routing. CI Cycle A workaround applied (haiku). Full provider setup needed. Acceptance: a) auth-profiles chmod 600, b) backup excludes auth files, c) Ollama Cloud provider configured, d) CI Cycle A reverted to deepseek-pro.
tickets.resolution: All 5 items complete. A: chmod 600 auth-profiles (CHG-0152). B: backup.sh excludes auth files (CHG-0154). C: Ollama Cloud models added to models.providers.ollama — kimi-k2.6, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash (CHG-0155). D: CI Cycle A reverted to deepseek-v4-pro:cloud (CHG-0155). E: cron-agent-preflight.sh updated with correct cloud model resolution logic — 5/5 crons PASS. HTTP API verified working via local Ollama Pro.

tickets.title: /handover cross-channel push broken
tickets.description: Telegram→webchat /handover always replies inline in Telegram instead of pushing to webchat. Step 4 of RULES.md (cron wake/sessions_send) is ignored. Partial fixes applied: visibility=agent config, RULES.md CRITICAL warning. Needs proper enforcement or alternative delivery mechanism.
tickets.resolution: Cross-channel /handover working. Fix: tools.sessions.visibility changed from 'tree' to 'agent' — allows Telegram session to see and reach webchat session via sessions_send. Confirmed by Ken 2026-05-04 23:23 AEST.

tickets.title: Content gating bypass — W1P1+ cron posts without confirmed Ken approval
tickets.description: Approval flow loophole: week1-posts.md status='Pending Ken approval' but W1P1/W1P2/W1P3 crons fire directly to LinkedIn API without checking approval state. Ken must reply APPROVED:[queue-id] per SPARK_RULES.md but no gate enforces this before posting. Risk: unapproved content published automatically. Fix: crons must check linkedin-queue.json for status=approved before posting, halt and alert if not.
tickets.resolution: Approval gate added to all 3 W1P crons (Step 0). Gate reads linkedin-queue.json, checks status=approved for the contentId, halts and alerts Ken via Telegram if not approved. Queue created with LI-W1-P1/P2/P3 all set to approved (Ken approved 2026-05-04 15:17 AEST).

Must be reviewed by Ken and approved before any external client signs. Referenced in AI Charter v1.0 Section 5.

tickets.notes: Deadline confirmed: 2026-08-02 (90 days from 2026-05-04, Ken confirmed). Mandatory before P2 live. Shield/Warden to define formal escalation thresholds, sensitivity levels, and suppression rules.

tickets.notes: Sequence: 2 of 4. Depends on TKT-0069 (Vision & Mission). Input to TKT-0071 roadmap. | Scope additions from AI governance gap analysis (2026-05-05): (1) Formalise risk reassessment cadence — quarterly periodic AI impact/risk assessment process (not just update when noticed). (2) Define structured decision log format aligned with what P2 external auditors expect. Lex to own the format. (3) HITL threshold table — enumerate specific monetary and data-access triggers per domain (not just qualitative tiers). These additions are required for P2 Evidence Pack completeness (Governance Framework v1.0 Section 6.5).

tickets.title: Nexus P1-P4 Roadmap — EA technology roadmap, blueprint and checklist
tickets.description: Enterprise Architect (Atlas) to produce technology roadmap, blueprint and checklist for P1 to P4. Covers strategy, progressive technology plans, policies, controls and audit gates per phase. TOGAF-aligned. Output: DRAFT FOR REVIEW document.
tickets.notes: Sequence: 3 of 4. Depends on TKT-0069 + TKT-0070. Owner: Atlas (Enterprise Architect). Output: DRAFT FOR REVIEW EA roadmap. | Sequence 3/4. Owner: Atlas (Enterprise Architect). ATLAS NOTE (2026-05-05): Ensure the P1-P4 roadmap includes a dedicated Technology Availability & Stability layer covering: (1) System availability architecture per phase — HA design, failover, recovery targets (RTO/RPO); (2) Observability stack — event loop monitoring, zombie task detection, gateway health, agent health, model performance trending; (3) AIOps maturity model — what checks exist per phase, what gets automated vs human-escalated; (4) Infrastructure resilience patterns — OC1/OC2 HA, Tailscale mesh, NAS failover; (5) SLA/SLO/error budget design per phase (P1 internal vs P2 client-facing vs P3/P4 enterprise); (6) Incident response capability per phase — runbooks, on-call model, escalation paths. Trigger: today's zombie task + event loop incident (INC CHG-0180) exposed gaps in our AIOps coverage. This layer must be a first-class section in the EA roadmap, not an afterthought.

tickets.title: SRE Agent — Krennic 🔵 (Site Reliability Engineering)
tickets.description: Build Krennic — SRE agent for AInchors platform. Owns incident response, SLO/error budget management, runbook execution, post-mortems, capacity planning, and toil reduction. Must be live BEFORE TRIGGER-07 (first P2 client). P1: design + spec. P2: activate. Activation also triggers if internal incident rate >2/week OR toil >30% of Yoda turns. Named after Director Orson Krennic — dedication, precision, uncompromising standards. Star Wars principle: commitment and craft, not allegiance.

tickets.title: Audit Log Architecture — Beacon v2: structured trace ID, tool-call logging, unified audit spine
tickets.description: P2 blocker. Gap from AI governance review (2026-05-05). Current state: two separate systems — tech logs (gateway.err.log) and audit logs (CHANGELOG.md + obs.db + Notion) — no bridge, no shared trace ID, no tool-call structured records, no prompt/response logging. Required: (1) Shared trace ID across multi-step agent runs. (2) Structured tool-call audit records: timestamp, tool name, params summary, outcome. (3) Policy event log: which guardrail fired, allow/block/modify decision, why. (4) Gateway + orchestrator + connector three-layer logging spine. (5) Same spine serves operational debugging AND compliance/regulatory reporting. Design owner: Atlas (EA) + Thrawn (PA). Build owner: Forge + Krennic (when live). Must be complete before P2 client onboarding.

tickets.title: AI Governance Framework v1.1 — S4 resolved, HITL thresholds, gateway PII control as P2 gate
tickets.description: Three updates required from gap analysis (2026-05-05): (1) S4 status — update YODA NOTE item 1 from 'known gap' to resolved. Document the tool scope matrix implemented CHG-0175 (2026-05-05). (2) HITL threshold table — add concrete triggers per domain to Charter (monetary thresholds, data-access triggers, new provider triggers). Currently qualitative tiers only — not sufficient for P2 audit. (3) Gateway-level PII control — add as a P2 pre-condition: lightweight automated PII pattern detection at the gateway layer before external API calls. Currently enforcement is config-level only (model routing). Infrastructure-level enforcement gap. Lex to assess and propose. Owner: Yoda. Requires Lex + Sage review before v1.1 approved.
